pages.html:
🎬 Watch the video above, then follow the steps below.

Make Money: Property Marketing Kits

What you'll have by the end

A finished property marketing kit — staged photos, an AI tour video, and a listing website — for a real property, plus a Firecrawl-scraped list of realtors with their contact info. That's a package you can sell at $250 to $1,000 per listing.

Why this matters

This is Build 3: the first play where Hermes stops costing you money and starts making it. Real estate is full of listings with photos that make it impossible to picture living there — and your agent can now fix that at scale.

Watch the video, then follow these steps

  1. Understand the offer first. Most listings have bland photos, empty rooms, and flat 2D floor plans. Your Hermes agent can now generate videos, generate photos, scrape the web with Firecrawl, and review its own work — so it can turn one ugly listing into a full marketing kit: a 3D-style designed floor plan you can actually "walk through," staged photos of empty rooms, landscaped versions of barren land, tour videos, and a listing website. Realtors love this, and you can charge $250–$1,000 per listing depending on how big it is.
  1. Grab the skill. In the community's skills vault, find the skill called property-marketing-kit. This is a Claude Code skill that handles the whole kit-building process for you. Copy it.
  1. Tell Hermes to install it in both places. Paste the skill in and tell your agent to install it in both Multica and Hermes, and to use it to build a marketing kit for a property.
  1. Pick a real property and send the full request as a goal. Samin uses an example New York listing. Give your agent the listing link plus the lead-scraping job, and mark it as a goal (remember /goal from Build 2 — this task takes a while, so it needs to run long):
Copy-paste prompt

Go find properties in New York, and for those apartments I want you to generate property marketing kits. Make sure you install the property-marketing-kit skill in both Multica and Hermes, and use it to build the kit.

Here's an example property: [paste listing link]. Build out a property marketing kit for this, please.

I also want you to generate a list of other properties — use Firecrawl to scrape them so I can send the kit to the realtors. Make sure you find the email address for the company hosting each listing, or a phone number, and also the property link. Get that back to me, please.

Make this a goal, because this is going to take a while.
  1. Let it run. This is a long job — walk away and come back when the agent reports in.
  1. Review the finished kit. When Hermes replies, it opens the kit folder in Finder. Samin's kit included staged photos of the property, "host" photos of him showing the place around (the skill automatically used his Higgsfield character), and tour videos — a bedroom clip and a kitchen clip with him presenting the space. It even built a kit for a second property (a Clearwater beachfront listing) complete with a listing website. Open the website and check it out — notice the vignette and small design touches. This is what you send to whoever you're selling to.
  1. Check your Telegram for the lead list. Hermes messages you a list of other properties it pulled with Firecrawl — each with a phone number and its RentHop listing link. Click through one and judge it yourself: even listings that already have a 3D tour can use better marketing when the rent is almost $6,000 a month.
  1. Notice the self-perpetuating loop. At the end, Hermes literally asks: *would you like me to generate a full marketing kit for all these listings next and start sending to these contacts?* It found the contacts on its own. Say yes and this runs on autopilot.
  1. Peek at Multica. Back in your Multica project you'll see the New York City property marketing kit alongside your earlier builds. Hermes even queued up the extra assets it might need to generate — without burning credits until you approve.
  1. Level it up (optional). You can build a skill so that every day Hermes finds new properties, builds new kits, and emails them to realtors with the offer: "want me to make more of these for you?" That's a self-running sales loop.

Copy-paste prompts

Go find properties in New York, and for those apartments I want you to generate property marketing kits. Make sure you install the property-marketing-kit skill in both Multica and Hermes, and use it to build the kit.

Here's an example property: [paste listing link]. Build out a property marketing kit for this, please.

I also want you to generate a list of other properties — use Firecrawl to scrape them so I can send the kit to the realtors. Make sure you find the email address for the company hosting each listing, or a phone number, and also the property link. Get that back to me, please.

Make this a goal, because this is going to take a while.

Troubleshooting

  • The AI "host" in your tour videos doesn't look like you. That happened to Samin too — his Higgsfield character just needed more training. Train it a bit more and regenerate.
  • Surprised the kit includes videos of "you"? That's built into the skill — it uses your Higgsfield setup automatically, even if you didn't ask for it.

✅ Your Day 11 post

Post a screenshot of your finished marketing kit (a staged photo, the tour video, or the listing website) plus the Telegram message with your scraped realtor lead list — your first sellable $250–$1,000 product.

We can see the Hermes 247 AI workforce website. Ah, this is the thing we built. Build a premium animated front end. All the stuff. And also the New York City property marketing kit. Awesome. So, it just Oh, that's really cool. It even queued up the different things it may have to make. I didn't tell it to do that, so I don't waste all my credits, but that's really cool. You can see how you can just set this on autopilot. Uh, and I guess that's why it's called autopilot. And you can make a skill here where every day it can find new properties, you can make new kits, and then send them these emails and make the offer, hey, can I make more for you? Would you pay me? So that's the bill three. It's super lucrative if you can do
this right because you literally have an engine that's going on autopilot that's potentially making you money, right?

rag.search_text:
Make Money: Property Marketing Kits

Day 11 — Make Money: Property Marketing Kits

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Make Money: Property Marketing Kits What you'll have by the end 

 A finished property marketing kit — staged photos, an AI tour video, and a listing website — for a real property, plus a Firecrawl-scraped list of realtors with their contact info. That's a package you can sell at $250 to $1,000 per listing .

 Why this matters 

 This is Build 3: the first play where Hermes stops costing you money and starts making it. Real estate is full of listings with photos that make it impossible to picture living there — and your agent can now fix that at scale.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 - Understand the offer first. Most listings have bland photos, empty rooms, and flat 2D floor plans. Your Hermes agent can now generate videos, generate photos, scrape the web with Firecrawl, and review its own work — so it can turn one ugly listing into a full marketing kit: a 3D-style designed floor plan you can actually "walk through," staged photos of empty rooms, landscaped versions of barren land, tour videos, and a listing website. Realtors love this, and you can charge $250–$1,000 per listing depending on how big it is.
 - Grab the skill. In the community's skills vault, find the skill called property-marketing-kit . This is a Claude Code skill that handles the whole kit-building process for you. Copy it.
 - Tell Hermes to install it in both places. Paste the skill in and tell your agent to install it in both Multica and Hermes , and to use it to build a marketing kit for a property.
 - Pick a real property and send the full request as a goal. Samin uses an example New York listing. Give your agent the listing link plus the lead-scraping job, and mark it as a goal (remember /goal from Build 2 — this task takes a while, so it needs to run long):
 Copy-paste prompt

Go find properties in New York, and for those apartments I want you to generate property marketing kits. Make sure you install the property-marketing-kit skill in both Multica and Hermes, and use it to build the kit.

Here's an example property: [paste listing link]. Build out a property marketing kit for this, please.

I also want you to generate a list of other properties — use Firecrawl to scrape them so I can send the kit to the realtors. Make sure you find the email address for the company hosting each listing, or a phone number, and also the property link. Get that back to me, please.

Make this a goal, because this is going to take a while. - Let it run. This is a long job — walk away and come back when the agent reports in.
 - Review the finished kit. When Hermes replies, it opens the kit folder in Finder. Samin's kit included staged photos of the property, "host" photos of him showing the place around (the skill automatically used his Higgsfield character), and tour videos — a bedroom clip and a kitchen clip with him presenting the space. It even built a kit for a second property (a Clearwater beachfront listing) complete with a listing website. Open the website and check it out — notice the vignette and small design touches. This is what you send to whoever you're selling to.
 - Check your Telegram for the lead list. Hermes messages you a list of other properties it pulled with Firecrawl — each with a phone number and its RentHop listing link. Click through one and judge it yourself: even listings that already have a 3D tour can use better marketing when the rent is almost $6,000 a month.
 - Notice the self-perpetuating loop. At the end, Hermes literally asks: *would you like me to generate a full marketing kit for all these listings next and start sending to these contacts?* It found the contacts on its own. Say yes and this runs on autopilot.
 - Peek at Multica. Back in your Multica project you'll see the New York City property marketing kit alongside your earlier builds. Hermes even queued up the extra assets it might need to generate — without burning credits until you approve.
 - Level it up (optional). You can build a skill so that every day Hermes finds new properties, builds new kits, and emails them to realtors with the offer: "want me to make more of these for you?" That's a self-running sales loop.
 Copy-paste prompts 

 Go find properties in New York, and for those apartments I want you to generate property marketing kits. Make sure you install the property-marketing-kit skill in both Multica and Hermes, and use it to build the kit.

Here's an example property: [paste listing link]. Build out a property marketing kit for this, please.

I also want you to generate a list of other properties — use Firecrawl to scrape them so I can send the kit to the realtors. Make sure you find the email address for the company hosting each listing, or a phone number, and also the property link. Get that back to me, please.

Make this a goal, because this is going to take a while. Troubleshooting 

 - The AI "host" in your tour videos doesn't look like you. That happened to Samin too — his Higgsfield character just needed more training. Train it a bit more and regenerate.
- Surprised the kit includes videos of "you"? That's built into the skill — it uses your Higgsfield setup automatically, even if you didn't ask for it.
 ✅ Your Day 11 post 

 Post a screenshot of your finished marketing kit (a staged photo, the tour video, or the listing website) plus the Telegram message with your scraped realtor lead list — your first sellable $250–$1,000 product.

pages.title: Social Posts That Publish Themselves
pages.folder: Day 12 — Social Posts That Publish Themselves
course_path: Day 12 — Social Posts That Publish Themselves
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Social Posts That Publish Themselves What you'll have by the end 

 A batch of designed carousels generated from a single prompt, plus a daily cross-platform posting calendar where the only thing you do is approve the output. Hermes researches, writes, designs, and schedules — you just say yes.

 Why this matters 

 There are only three ways to get clients: free content, outreach, or paid ads. This build makes the first one run on autopilot, so your personal brand grows while you do other work.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 - Understand the game plan first. To get clients for any business, you have three options: post free content (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, YouTube), do outreach (send prospects something you made for them, like the marketing kits from the last build), or run paid ads. This chapter builds option one.
 - Start with carousels. They're the common format across every platform — Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn all support them. They're high reach, easy to reproduce, and very scalable for Hermes. Every piece of content follows the same workflow: research trends and keywords, write the script, create it, edit, post, analyze. Hermes will handle all of that.
 - Make a Twitter-style carousel. Grab the Instagram thread carousel skill Samin made for you (it's in the community — he shows exactly where in the video). Copy it, open your Hermes app, and paste it in. Then also paste in a headshot of yourself so it can put your face and name on the slides. Prompt it:
 Copy-paste prompt
Fable 5 for Claude just came out. Can you create a carousel about Fable 5 and what that means, using this Instagram thread carousel skill. I've pasted my headshot — use that. Swap "Fable 5" for whatever topic you want. This style is cheap to run — no image model needed, it's mostly code and research.

 - Check the output in artifacts. Anything your Hermes agent generates gets stored in the artifacts area, so you can preview every carousel it made in one place.
 - Make a designed pixel-style carousel. Download the pixel carousel style skill (again, from where Samin shows on screen), then drag the file into Hermes and prompt:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you use this pixel style skill — download this into yourself — and then create a carousel about [your topic]. Use Higgsfield if you need to generate any images. This one uses the Higgsfield MCP you connected earlier in the course, so Hermes can generate the graphics itself.

 - Clone any carousel style you like. This is the real superpower. See a carousel style anywhere online? Screenshot it, drop the image into Hermes, and say:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you make a skill in this style? Hermes plus Higgsfield will turn that screenshot into a reusable skill. From then on, just tell it to use that skill to generate carousels — any style, forever.

 - Put generation on autopilot. Set the carousel generation up as a cron job (or in Multica, like Samin's "Fable 5 carousel campaign" project shown in the video). Now Hermes does the research and writing every single day — all you do is approve.
 - Auto-post, path one: Composio. You're already authenticated with Composio from earlier. Go find Instagram in Composio, connect your account, and connect any others you use — TikTok, Facebook, YouTube are all in there.
 - Auto-post, path two: Blotato (Samin's pick). Blotato gives you one MCP that talks to Facebook, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and X at once, plus a calendar view of everything scheduled and built-in media storage. In Blotato, go to the API section, copy your key, then tell Hermes:
 Copy-paste prompt
Hey, can you help me authenticate with my Blotato MCP? - Once connected, test it:
 Copy-paste prompt
Post my latest carousel to Instagram. Then set the schedule:

 Copy-paste prompt
Schedule one carousel every day. Hermes will build a content calendar in Blotato, posting across all your socials, and you can manage everything from the calendar view.

 Copy-paste prompts 

 Fable 5 for Claude just came out. Can you create a carousel about Fable 5 and what that means, using this Instagram thread carousel skill. I've pasted my headshot — use that. Can you use this pixel style skill — download this into yourself — and then create a carousel about [your topic]. Use Higgsfield if you need to generate any images. Can you make a skill in this style? Hey, can you help me authenticate with my Blotato MCP? Post my latest carousel to Instagram. Schedule one carousel every day. Troubleshooting 

 Blotato's OAuth doesn't work for the MCP connection. Instead, scroll down on the Blotato page and hit Copy MCP config , then paste that into Hermes. It should connect right after that.

 ✅ Your Day 12 post 

 Post a screenshot of a carousel batch Hermes generated for you — bonus points if you also show your Blotato (or Composio) posting calendar with posts scheduled.

Then set the schedule:

rag.search_text:
Social Posts That Publish Themselves

Day 12 — Social Posts That Publish Themselves

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Social Posts That Publish Themselves What you'll have by the end 

 A batch of designed carousels generated from a single prompt, plus a daily cross-platform posting calendar where the only thing you do is approve the output. Hermes researches, writes, designs, and schedules — you just say yes.

 Why this matters 

 There are only three ways to get clients: free content, outreach, or paid ads. This build makes the first one run on autopilot, so your personal brand grows while you do other work.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 - Understand the game plan first. To get clients for any business, you have three options: post free content (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, YouTube), do outreach (send prospects something you made for them, like the marketing kits from the last build), or run paid ads. This chapter builds option one.
 - Start with carousels. They're the common format across every platform — Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn all support them. They're high reach, easy to reproduce, and very scalable for Hermes. Every piece of content follows the same workflow: research trends and keywords, write the script, create it, edit, post, analyze. Hermes will handle all of that.
 - Make a Twitter-style carousel. Grab the Instagram thread carousel skill Samin made for you (it's in the community — he shows exactly where in the video). Copy it, open your Hermes app, and paste it in. Then also paste in a headshot of yourself so it can put your face and name on the slides. Prompt it:
 Copy-paste prompt
Fable 5 for Claude just came out. Can you create a carousel about Fable 5 and what that means, using this Instagram thread carousel skill. I've pasted my headshot — use that. Swap "Fable 5" for whatever topic you want. This style is cheap to run — no image model needed, it's mostly code and research.

 - Check the output in artifacts. Anything your Hermes agent generates gets stored in the artifacts area, so you can preview every carousel it made in one place.
 - Make a designed pixel-style carousel. Download the pixel carousel style skill (again, from where Samin shows on screen), then drag the file into Hermes and prompt:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you use this pixel style skill — download this into yourself — and then create a carousel about [your topic]. Use Higgsfield if you need to generate any images. This one uses the Higgsfield MCP you connected earlier in the course, so Hermes can generate the graphics itself.

 - Clone any carousel style you like. This is the real superpower. See a carousel style anywhere online? Screenshot it, drop the image into Hermes, and say:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you make a skill in this style? Hermes plus Higgsfield will turn that screenshot into a reusable skill. From then on, just tell it to use that skill to generate carousels — any style, forever.

 - Put generation on autopilot. Set the carousel generation up as a cron job (or in Multica, like Samin's "Fable 5 carousel campaign" project shown in the video). Now Hermes does the research and writing every single day — all you do is approve.
 - Auto-post, path one: Composio. You're already authenticated with Composio from earlier. Go find Instagram in Composio, connect your account, and connect any others you use — TikTok, Facebook, YouTube are all in there.
 - Auto-post, path two: Blotato (Samin's pick). Blotato gives you one MCP that talks to Facebook, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and X at once, plus a calendar view of everything scheduled and built-in media storage. In Blotato, go to the API section, copy your key, then tell Hermes:
 Copy-paste prompt
Hey, can you help me authenticate with my Blotato MCP? - Once connected, test it:
 Copy-paste prompt
Post my latest carousel to Instagram. Then set the schedule:

 Copy-paste prompt
Schedule one carousel every day. Hermes will build a content calendar in Blotato, posting across all your socials, and you can manage everything from the calendar view.

 Copy-paste prompts 

 Fable 5 for Claude just came out. Can you create a carousel about Fable 5 and what that means, using this Instagram thread carousel skill. I've pasted my headshot — use that. Can you use this pixel style skill — download this into yourself — and then create a carousel about [your topic]. Use Higgsfield if you need to generate any images. Can you make a skill in this style? Hey, can you help me authenticate with my Blotato MCP? Post my latest carousel to Instagram. Schedule one carousel every day. Troubleshooting 

 Blotato's OAuth doesn't work for the MCP connection. Instead, scroll down on the Blotato page and hit Copy MCP config , then paste that into Hermes. It should connect right after that.

 ✅ Your Day 12 post 

 Post a screenshot of a carousel batch Hermes generated for you — bonus points if you also show your Blotato (or Composio) posting calendar with posts scheduled.

- pixel-pal-carousel — The downloadable pixel-style designed carousel skill shown as the second carousel format. — https://github.com/Samin12/pixel-pal-carousel 
- linkedin-pdf-carousel — Same carousel engine adapted for LinkedIn PDF carousels — useful for the cross-platform posting calendar built here. — https://github.com/Samin12/linkedin-pdf-carousel

pages.title: Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content
pages.folder: Day 13 — Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content
course_path: Day 13 — Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content What you'll have by the end 

 One YouTube video repurposed into a full content package — clips, tweets, X threads, LinkedIn posts, a lead magnet, an SEO blog, and reel scripts — plus one raw clip you filmed yourself, auto-edited by Hermes into a polished reel with B-roll, music, and captions.

 Why this matters 

 This is the content octopus: one long-form video feeds every other platform, so you grow everywhere at the same time without doing the work yourself. Samin calls this his single highest-leverage system — and it's one you can even sell to clients.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 Part 1 — Repurpose one YouTube video into everything 

 - Go to your YouTube channel and pick any video. Copy its link.
 - Open Hermes and paste the link in.

pages.html:
🎬 Watch the video above, then follow the steps below.

Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content

What you'll have by the end

One YouTube video repurposed into a full content package — clips, tweets, X threads, LinkedIn posts, a lead magnet, an SEO blog, and reel scripts — plus one raw clip you filmed yourself, auto-edited by Hermes into a polished reel with B-roll, music, and captions.

Why this matters

This is the content octopus: one long-form video feeds every other platform, so you grow everywhere at the same time without doing the work yourself. Samin calls this his single highest-leverage system — and it's one you can even sell to clients.

Watch the video, then follow these steps

Part 1 — Repurpose one YouTube video into everything

  1. Go to your YouTube channel and pick any video. Copy its link.
  1. Open Hermes and paste the link in.
  1. Go to your Skool community, open the Classroom, and find the content repurpose skill. Copy it.
  1. Go back to Hermes, paste the skill in with the video link, and send this:

Copy-paste prompt:

Hey, I just gave you a skill. I want you to download and install that skill, and after that use that skill to make me a dashboard of the content repurpose system, so I can use all of those and make my content a lot easier. Basically, activate the system on this video.
  1. Let it run — this takes a while. When it's done, Hermes will have run the entire workflow and dropped everything into a folder.
  1. Look through what it made. In Samin's run, one video became: short clips, a long-form SEO blog, eight tweets, a couple of X threads, a LinkedIn post, a LinkedIn lead magnet, a Skool guide (paste it into your email list and blast it out for extra views), long articles, and short-form reel scripts — each with a new hook and a ready-made caption so you can re-film parts of the video and squeeze even more reels out of it.
  1. Important: make sure Hermes is connected to Higgsfield (the MCP you set up earlier). The system generates a lot of pictures and uses Higgsfield during the clipping too.

Part 2 — Film a reel script and let AI edit it

  1. Pick one of the generated reel scripts (Samin films "clip 3" — the one about creating a Higgsfield Soul). Film yourself reading it. Don't worry about being perfect: retakes, ums, and pauses are fine. The AI will cut them. At the end of your recording, say out loud what B-roll or screen recording you plan to show — that helps the agent place it.
  1. AI video editing happens in two passes: first pass cuts out the bad takes, ums, ahs, and pauses. Second pass finds B-roll and inserts it into the final polished video. You'll use two skills for this — the cut video skill and the B-roll finder / motion graphics skill.
  1. Open a new session in Hermes and insert both skills (grab them from the Skool classroom, same as before). Hermes chains both passes together using the /goal command.
  1. Drag your raw clip file into Hermes, then send this:

Copy-paste prompt:

Edit this reel. I have a lot of bad cuts. Make a goal that you will give me the final video. For the first pass, go through and use the cut video skill to cut out all the ums, ahs, bad takes, etc. After that is done, use the second skill to find B-roll and insert it in. Make sure I am on the bottom and the B-roll is on the top, so it looks a little nicer. At the end of the recording, I say what to insert — find and attach that clip of me from the YouTube video I've linked.
  1. Paste the original YouTube video link in as a third attachment, so the agent can pull B-roll of you straight from it. Then let it run — this takes a while.
  1. When it finishes, you'll get cut one (bad takes removed) and cut two (the final version with B-roll on top, you on the bottom, plus music and captions added automatically).
  1. Check your project — the finished reel lands in a review queue. This is your one place to track every piece of content before it goes out, so you can film a batch of reels and publish them all from one spot.

Copy-paste prompts

Install and run the repurpose system:

Hey, I just gave you a skill. I want you to download and install that skill, and after that use that skill to make me a dashboard of the content repurpose system, so I can use all of those and make my content a lot easier. Basically, activate the system on this video.

Two-pass reel edit:

Edit this reel. I have a lot of bad cuts. Make a goal that you will give me the final video. For the first pass, go through and use the cut video skill to cut out all the ums, ahs, bad takes, etc. After that is done, use the second skill to find B-roll and insert it in. Make sure I am on the bottom and the B-roll is on the top, so it looks a little nicer. At the end of the recording, I say what to insert — find and attach that clip of me from the YouTube video I've linked.

Troubleshooting

  • Rough edges on the final reel (like black bars): normal on the first output. Samin's fix is simple — send it through one more pass and it's pretty much good to go.
  • Image or clip generation failing: check that Higgsfield is connected. The repurpose system leans on it for pictures and clipping.

✅ Your Day 13 post

Post a screenshot of your generated content package (the folder of clips, tweets, and posts) — and if you filmed one, attach your auto-edited reel with captions and B-roll.

rag.search_text:
Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content

Day 13 — Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Turn One Video Into 30 Pieces of Content What you'll have by the end 

 One YouTube video repurposed into a full content package — clips, tweets, X threads, LinkedIn posts, a lead magnet, an SEO blog, and reel scripts — plus one raw clip you filmed yourself, auto-edited by Hermes into a polished reel with B-roll, music, and captions.

 Why this matters 

 This is the content octopus: one long-form video feeds every other platform, so you grow everywhere at the same time without doing the work yourself. Samin calls this his single highest-leverage system — and it's one you can even sell to clients.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 Part 1 — Repurpose one YouTube video into everything 

 - Go to your YouTube channel and pick any video. Copy its link.
 - Open Hermes and paste the link in.
 - Go to your Skool community, open the Classroom , and find the content repurpose skill . Copy it.
 - Go back to Hermes, paste the skill in with the video link, and send this:
 Copy-paste prompt:

 Hey, I just gave you a skill. I want you to download and install that skill, and after that use that skill to make me a dashboard of the content repurpose system, so I can use all of those and make my content a lot easier. Basically, activate the system on this video. - Let it run — this takes a while. When it's done, Hermes will have run the entire workflow and dropped everything into a folder.
 - Look through what it made. In Samin's run, one video became: short clips, a long-form SEO blog, eight tweets, a couple of X threads, a LinkedIn post, a LinkedIn lead magnet, a Skool guide (paste it into your email list and blast it out for extra views), long articles, and short-form reel scripts — each with a new hook and a ready-made caption so you can re-film parts of the video and squeeze even more reels out of it.
 - Important: make sure Hermes is connected to Higgsfield (the MCP you set up earlier). The system generates a lot of pictures and uses Higgsfield during the clipping too.
 Part 2 — Film a reel script and let AI edit it 

 - Pick one of the generated reel scripts (Samin films "clip 3" — the one about creating a Higgsfield Soul). Film yourself reading it. Don't worry about being perfect: retakes, ums, and pauses are fine. The AI will cut them. At the end of your recording, say out loud what B-roll or screen recording you plan to show — that helps the agent place it.
 - AI video editing happens in two passes : first pass cuts out the bad takes, ums, ahs, and pauses. Second pass finds B-roll and inserts it into the final polished video. You'll use two skills for this — the cut video skill and the B-roll finder / motion graphics skill .
 - Open a new session in Hermes and insert both skills (grab them from the Skool classroom, same as before). Hermes chains both passes together using the /goal command.
 - Drag your raw clip file into Hermes, then send this:
 Copy-paste prompt:

 Edit this reel. I have a lot of bad cuts. Make a goal that you will give me the final video. For the first pass, go through and use the cut video skill to cut out all the ums, ahs, bad takes, etc. After that is done, use the second skill to find B-roll and insert it in. Make sure I am on the bottom and the B-roll is on the top, so it looks a little nicer. At the end of the recording, I say what to insert — find and attach that clip of me from the YouTube video I've linked. - Paste the original YouTube video link in as a third attachment, so the agent can pull B-roll of you straight from it. Then let it run — this takes a while.
 - When it finishes, you'll get cut one (bad takes removed) and cut two (the final version with B-roll on top, you on the bottom, plus music and captions added automatically).
 - Check your project — the finished reel lands in a review queue . This is your one place to track every piece of content before it goes out, so you can film a batch of reels and publish them all from one spot.
 Copy-paste prompts 

 Install and run the repurpose system:

 Hey, I just gave you a skill. I want you to download and install that skill, and after that use that skill to make me a dashboard of the content repurpose system, so I can use all of those and make my content a lot easier. Basically, activate the system on this video. Two-pass reel edit:

 Edit this reel. I have a lot of bad cuts. Make a goal that you will give me the final video. For the first pass, go through and use the cut video skill to cut out all the ums, ahs, bad takes, etc. After that is done, use the second skill to find B-roll and insert it in. Make sure I am on the bottom and the B-roll is on the top, so it looks a little nicer. At the end of the recording, I say what to insert — find and attach that clip of me from the YouTube video I've linked. Troubleshooting 

 - Rough edges on the final reel (like black bars): normal on the first output. Samin's fix is simple — send it through one more pass and it's pretty much good to go.
- Image or clip generation failing: check that Higgsfield is connected. The repurpose system leans on it for pictures and clipping.
 ✅ Your Day 13 post 

 Post a screenshot of your generated content package (the folder of clips, tweets, and posts) — and if you filmed one, attach your auto-edited reel with captions and B-roll.

pages.title: Generate Product Ads in Minutes
pages.folder: Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Generate Product Ads in Minutes What you'll have by the end: A finished set of realistic product ad images and a product video — generated from one photo of your product and one prompt — ready to launch.

 Why this matters: If you sell a product but don't do talking-head videos, this is your content machine. One photo in, a full batch of high-converting ad creatives out — and once it works, you can put it on autopilot.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps This build uses the same pieces you've already set up: Hermes, your Higgsfield Soul, and a skill. The only new ingredient is a photo of your product.

 - Take a photo of your product. Any product works — in the video, Samin uses a pink drink. Just snap a clear picture of it.
 - Grab the ads skill. Copy the ecom ads skill from the resources, just like you've done with skills in earlier chapters.
 - Open Hermes and start a new session. Paste the skill in.
 - Tell Hermes what you want. Type or dictate the prompt below. Notice two key parts: you're telling it to use the skill for the whole creative process, and you're telling it to use your Higgsfield Soul so *you* appear in the video ads.
 Copy-paste prompt

Download the skill. For the product photo I'm going to give you, create some image and video ads. Make sure the video ads have me in them — I have a Higgsfield Soul that you can refer to. Use that Soul to put me in the videos and make these ads. Go through the whole creative process and use the skill to come up with all the different creatives. - Attach your product photo. Add the picture you took in step 1 as a reference image in the same message, then send it.
 - Let it work. Behind the scenes, Hermes clones the ecom skill, researches competitor ads, and runs the full creative process. When it's done, it opens a folder with everything it made.
 - Review the creatives. Open the outputs together and look through them. In the video, Hermes produced a product video plus a set of image ads: a realistic-looking woman in the shot, benefit-driven copy like "Pick your pink," a funny "emotional support" angle, and a contrast shot showing off the colors. All from one photo.
 - Check Multica. Go to your project in Multica and you'll see the whole batch there too (in the video it shows up as a "pink drink ads" project), so you can browse everything Hermes created in one place.
 - Scale it (the big unlock). Once you like the output, two upgrades turn this into an ad factory: set a cron job so Hermes makes fresh ads on autopilot, and connect the Meta Ads MCP so Hermes can keep launching them and see which ones actually perform. That's how you get to hundreds of ads a day with Hermes managing the whole thing.
 Copy-paste prompts Product ads from one photo (attach your product photo with it): 

 Download the skill. For the product photo I'm going to give you, create some image and video ads. Make sure the video ads have me in them — I have a Higgsfield Soul that you can refer to. Use that Soul to put me in the videos and make these ads. Go through the whole creative process and use the skill to come up with all the different creatives. ✅ Your Day 14 post Post your favorite ad image (or the product video) that Hermes generated from your one product photo — and tell us what the product is.

pages.folder_normalized: Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent
pages.location_label: Hermes Agent Course [BETA] → Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent → Generate Product Ads in Minutes

rag.search_text:
Generate Product Ads in Minutes

Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Generate Product Ads in Minutes What you'll have by the end: A finished set of realistic product ad images and a product video — generated from one photo of your product and one prompt — ready to launch.

 Why this matters: If you sell a product but don't do talking-head videos, this is your content machine. One photo in, a full batch of high-converting ad creatives out — and once it works, you can put it on autopilot.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps This build uses the same pieces you've already set up: Hermes, your Higgsfield Soul, and a skill. The only new ingredient is a photo of your product.

 - Take a photo of your product. Any product works — in the video, Samin uses a pink drink. Just snap a clear picture of it.
 - Grab the ads skill. Copy the ecom ads skill from the resources, just like you've done with skills in earlier chapters.
 - Open Hermes and start a new session. Paste the skill in.
 - Tell Hermes what you want. Type or dictate the prompt below. Notice two key parts: you're telling it to use the skill for the whole creative process, and you're telling it to use your Higgsfield Soul so *you* appear in the video ads.
 Copy-paste prompt

Download the skill. For the product photo I'm going to give you, create some image and video ads. Make sure the video ads have me in them — I have a Higgsfield Soul that you can refer to. Use that Soul to put me in the videos and make these ads. Go through the whole creative process and use the skill to come up with all the different creatives. - Attach your product photo. Add the picture you took in step 1 as a reference image in the same message, then send it.
 - Let it work. Behind the scenes, Hermes clones the ecom skill, researches competitor ads, and runs the full creative process. When it's done, it opens a folder with everything it made.
 - Review the creatives. Open the outputs together and look through them. In the video, Hermes produced a product video plus a set of image ads: a realistic-looking woman in the shot, benefit-driven copy like "Pick your pink," a funny "emotional support" angle, and a contrast shot showing off the colors. All from one photo.
 - Check Multica. Go to your project in Multica and you'll see the whole batch there too (in the video it shows up as a "pink drink ads" project), so you can browse everything Hermes created in one place.
 - Scale it (the big unlock). Once you like the output, two upgrades turn this into an ad factory: set a cron job so Hermes makes fresh ads on autopilot, and connect the Meta Ads MCP so Hermes can keep launching them and see which ones actually perform. That's how you get to hundreds of ads a day with Hermes managing the whole thing.
 Copy-paste prompts Product ads from one photo (attach your product photo with it): 

 Download the skill. For the product photo I'm going to give you, create some image and video ads. Make sure the video ads have me in them — I have a Higgsfield Soul that you can refer to. Use that Soul to put me in the videos and make these ads. Go through the whole creative process and use the skill to come up with all the different creatives. ✅ Your Day 14 post Post your favorite ad image (or the product video) that Hermes generated from your one product photo — and tell us what the product is.

pages.title: 24/7 Customer Support on Autopilot
pages.folder: Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 24/7 Customer Support on Autopilot What you'll have by the end: A community manager agent that checks your Skool (or Discord, or wherever your people hang out) three times a day — answering questions, keeping tabs on posts, and sending you reports. Plus, your Hermes agent gets its own live email address and sends its first email to prove it works.

 Why this matters: Once you're making content and landing clients, support becomes the bottleneck. This build means members get faster, more detailed answers even when you're asleep — and customers can email your agent directly.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps First, watch what's possible. Samin shows his live Hermes bot inside his own Skool community: it answers members' questions in the comments, gives people prompts, builds them shareable dashboards, and he even has a second bot posting trade alerts. He can also ask it things like "what's the sentiment of everyone here?" and get a real answer. Now build your own.

 1. Create a dedicated account for your bot. Make a brand-new Skool account (or an account on whatever platform your community lives on — Discord works the same way). This is the bot's account, not your personal account .

 2. Understand the plan before you run it. The build has three parts: give the agent the bot account's credentials, tell it its job (check in daily, learn from the community, answer questions), and set up cron jobs so it runs on a schedule — a morning check, a midday reply sweep, and an evening community summary, plus a weekly report on what people have been asking.

 3. Copy the pre-written prompt. Samin keeps this one in the agent teams because it's long. Grab the full "School Manager Agent" prompt from the course resources — it tells Hermes to build the community manager, use the dedicated account's credentials, defines its job, sets the three daily cron jobs and the weekly learning report, and includes rules and example behavior.

 4. Paste it into Hermes and hit go. Open your Hermes agent, start a new session, paste the whole prompt, and let it work. When it finishes, check Multica: in your project you should see the school manager agent is ready .

 5. Set the check-in schedule. Tell the agent something like this:

 Copy-paste prompt

Set up recurring cron jobs to check my community at 9am, 2pm, and 5pm every day. Three sweeps a day is Samin's setup, but it's up to you — configure more check-ins if you want them.

 6. Give your agent its own email inbox with AgentMail. Go to agentmail.to and log in. Then:

 - Create an inbox — give it a name (Samin uses a test name like "test Hermes"). You can choose any domain, or add your own domain.
- Create an API key (name it something like "test Hermes") and copy it .
 7. Onboard Hermes to its new inbox. Go back to Hermes, start a new session, give it the inbox details and the API key, and tell it:

 Copy-paste prompt

This is your inbox. Here are your API keys: [paste your AgentMail API key]. Can you onboard to it, and then send me an email as a test? My email is [your email address]. 8. Check your email. Within a couple of minutes you should get a message from your agent's new address. Samin's read: *"Hey Samin, good news, I have my own email address now. If you ever need anything done, you can email me here or forward me something you want me to take care of. I can help review, summarize, draft, organize, follow up, or turn things into tasks."* You can also see the sent message inside AgentMail's inbox view.

 That's it — your agent now monitors your community on a schedule and has a real email address. Customers can email it for support, or you can forward it anything you want handled.

 Copy-paste prompts Community check-in schedule: 

 Set up recurring cron jobs to check my community at 9am, 2pm, and 5pm every day. AgentMail onboarding + test email: 

 This is your inbox. Here are your API keys: [paste your AgentMail API key]. Can you onboard to it, and then send me an email as a test? My email is [your email address]. School Manager Agent build prompt: this one is long — grab the full version from the course resources, paste it into a new Hermes session, and hit go. (It covers: use the dedicated account's credentials, define the job, morning check + midday reply sweep + evening summary cron jobs, weekly learning report, rules, and example behavior.)

 ✅ Your Day 14 post Post a screenshot of the test email your agent sent you from its own AgentMail address — or a screenshot of your community manager's cron schedule confirmed in Hermes.

rag.search_text:
24/7 Customer Support on Autopilot

Day 14 — Ads & Support Agent

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 24/7 Customer Support on Autopilot What you'll have by the end: A community manager agent that checks your Skool (or Discord, or wherever your people hang out) three times a day — answering questions, keeping tabs on posts, and sending you reports. Plus, your Hermes agent gets its own live email address and sends its first email to prove it works.

 Why this matters: Once you're making content and landing clients, support becomes the bottleneck. This build means members get faster, more detailed answers even when you're asleep — and customers can email your agent directly.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps First, watch what's possible. Samin shows his live Hermes bot inside his own Skool community: it answers members' questions in the comments, gives people prompts, builds them shareable dashboards, and he even has a second bot posting trade alerts. He can also ask it things like "what's the sentiment of everyone here?" and get a real answer. Now build your own.

 1. Create a dedicated account for your bot. Make a brand-new Skool account (or an account on whatever platform your community lives on — Discord works the same way). This is the bot's account, not your personal account .

 2. Understand the plan before you run it. The build has three parts: give the agent the bot account's credentials, tell it its job (check in daily, learn from the community, answer questions), and set up cron jobs so it runs on a schedule — a morning check, a midday reply sweep, and an evening community summary, plus a weekly report on what people have been asking.

 3. Copy the pre-written prompt. Samin keeps this one in the agent teams because it's long. Grab the full "School Manager Agent" prompt from the course resources — it tells Hermes to build the community manager, use the dedicated account's credentials, defines its job, sets the three daily cron jobs and the weekly learning report, and includes rules and example behavior.

 4. Paste it into Hermes and hit go. Open your Hermes agent, start a new session, paste the whole prompt, and let it work. When it finishes, check Multica: in your project you should see the school manager agent is ready .

 5. Set the check-in schedule. Tell the agent something like this:

 Copy-paste prompt

Set up recurring cron jobs to check my community at 9am, 2pm, and 5pm every day. Three sweeps a day is Samin's setup, but it's up to you — configure more check-ins if you want them.

 6. Give your agent its own email inbox with AgentMail. Go to agentmail.to and log in. Then:

 - Create an inbox — give it a name (Samin uses a test name like "test Hermes"). You can choose any domain, or add your own domain.
- Create an API key (name it something like "test Hermes") and copy it .
 7. Onboard Hermes to its new inbox. Go back to Hermes, start a new session, give it the inbox details and the API key, and tell it:

 Copy-paste prompt

This is your inbox. Here are your API keys: [paste your AgentMail API key]. Can you onboard to it, and then send me an email as a test? My email is [your email address]. 8. Check your email. Within a couple of minutes you should get a message from your agent's new address. Samin's read: *"Hey Samin, good news, I have my own email address now. If you ever need anything done, you can email me here or forward me something you want me to take care of. I can help review, summarize, draft, organize, follow up, or turn things into tasks."* You can also see the sent message inside AgentMail's inbox view.

 That's it — your agent now monitors your community on a schedule and has a real email address. Customers can email it for support, or you can forward it anything you want handled.

 Copy-paste prompts Community check-in schedule: 

 Set up recurring cron jobs to check my community at 9am, 2pm, and 5pm every day. AgentMail onboarding + test email: 

 This is your inbox. Here are your API keys: [paste your AgentMail API key]. Can you onboard to it, and then send me an email as a test? My email is [your email address]. School Manager Agent build prompt: this one is long — grab the full version from the course resources, paste it into a new Hermes session, and hit go. (It covers: use the dedicated account's credentials, define the job, morning check + midday reply sweep + evening summary cron jobs, weekly learning report, rules, and example behavior.)

 ✅ Your Day 14 post Post a screenshot of the test email your agent sent you from its own AgentMail address — or a screenshot of your community manager's cron schedule confirmed in Hermes.

pages.title: Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot
pages.folder: Day 15 — Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot
course_path: Day 15 — Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot What you'll have by the end: An automated paper-trading bot that copy-trades a proven strategy with $10,000 in fake money — polling QuiverQuant for new trades every day, mirroring them through Coinvest, and publishing a live portfolio dashboard on here.now every Friday.

 Why this matters: Hermes learns from its own past trades, so its knowledge compounds — the bot literally gets better the longer it runs. And because this is paper trading, you get all the reps with zero risk.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps A trading bot only needs two services: a broker (Coinvest) and a strategy (QuiverQuant). Hermes glues them together and runs the cron jobs. Here's the exact build.

 - Set up your broker. Go to CoInvest.ai and log in — it's free to make an account. Coinvest lets you trade stocks, crypto, forex, even prediction markets (yes, you can bet on the World Cup), all in one platform built for agentic trading.
 - Grab the connection info. On Coinvest, hit Start on Claude and copy what it gives you (this is the OAuth setup for the Coinvest MCP).
 - Connect it through Telegram. Open the multi-channel Telegram chat you set up earlier and go to your trading topic. Samin does this in Telegram on purpose — you want to be nimble, texting your bot and getting alerts on the go. Paste what you copied and send:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here. - Authorize it. A window pops up — hit Connect to Coinvest . Then make sure paper trading is enabled so nothing here is real money.
 - Test the connection. Text your bot: "Hey, what's in my portfolio right now?" You should get back a mock portfolio — stocks, crypto, gold, the works. That means you're connected.
 - Set up your live dashboard habit. Ask Hermes: "Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now?" This is the exact standing instruction Samin gives his real bot — every time it responds and does something, it builds a dashboard and hands you the link.
 - Get your strategy from QuiverQuant. Log into your QuiverQuant account. Here you can see all congressional trading — which sitting politicians made which trades. (Context from the video: Elizabeth Warren has a $67M net worth on a $285K salary — that's 225 years of saving every paycheck. Nancy Pelosi has outperformed the stock market by a massive margin.) Samin's favorite spot is the Strategies folder — it tracks traders like Michael Burry and Bill Ackman too, and you can filter performance by 1 month, 3 months, or 6 months. Some strategies show a 57.8% return over the last three months. The play: copy-trade them.
 - Hook up the QuiverQuant MCP. In QuiverQuant, copy your API token (keep it private — don't screen-share it), then go to MCP servers and copy that setup too. Back in Telegram, send it to Hermes along with your API key:
 Copy-paste prompt
Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY] Once connected, Hermes will confirm it can pull real congressional trade data, follow specific politicians, copy-trade QuiverQuant's built-in strategies (like Congress Buy), run deeper research, and set up recurring jobs.

 - Pick a trader to mirror. Go back to QuiverQuant's Strategies folder and find someone performing well over the last three months. Samin picks Tim Moore . Pick whoever looks strong when you watch.
 - Launch the full bot. Send Hermes the big one:
 Copy-paste prompt
Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of Tim Moore. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading Tim Moore, we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project. - Verify the build. Hermes should confirm: a $10K paper portfolio copy-trading Tim Moore via the QuiverQuant MCP, a daily polling cron, a Friday dashboard cron, and trades mirrored with a 1-3 day disclosure lag. Open the here.now dashboard link and check that it's live.
 That's the whole bot. If you ever want to go live with real money, you can flip paper trading off in Coinvest — but run the paper experiment first and let the results talk.

 Copy-paste prompts Connect Coinvest: 

 Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here. Test it: 

 Hey, what's in my portfolio right now? Dashboard habit: 

 Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now? Connect QuiverQuant: 

 Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY] Launch the bot: 

 Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of [TRADER NAME]. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading [TRADER NAME], we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project. Troubleshooting Your API key is visible in the chat. After sending your QuiverQuant key to Hermes, Samin deletes that message from Telegram so the key isn't sitting in the thread. Do the same — and never show your token on a screenshot or screen-share.

 Double-check paper trading is on. Before you send any trading prompts, confirm paper trading is enabled in Coinvest. Everything in this lesson should be fake money until you deliberately decide otherwise.

 ✅ Your Day 15 post Post a screenshot of your live here.now trading dashboard showing your $10K paper account and the trader you're copy-trading — and tell us who you picked and why.

🎬 Watch the video above, then follow the steps below.

Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot

What you'll have by the end: An automated paper-trading bot that copy-trades a proven strategy with $10,000 in fake money — polling QuiverQuant for new trades every day, mirroring them through Coinvest, and publishing a live portfolio dashboard on here.now every Friday.

Why this matters: Hermes learns from its own past trades, so its knowledge compounds — the bot literally gets better the longer it runs. And because this is paper trading, you get all the reps with zero risk.

Watch the video, then follow these steps

A trading bot only needs two services: a broker (Coinvest) and a strategy (QuiverQuant). Hermes glues them together and runs the cron jobs. Here's the exact build.

  1. Set up your broker. Go to CoInvest.ai and log in — it's free to make an account. Coinvest lets you trade stocks, crypto, forex, even prediction markets (yes, you can bet on the World Cup), all in one platform built for agentic trading.
  1. Grab the connection info. On Coinvest, hit Start on Claude and copy what it gives you (this is the OAuth setup for the Coinvest MCP).
  1. Connect it through Telegram. Open the multi-channel Telegram chat you set up earlier and go to your trading topic. Samin does this in Telegram on purpose — you want to be nimble, texting your bot and getting alerts on the go. Paste what you copied and send:
Copy-paste prompt
Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here.
  1. Authorize it. A window pops up — hit Connect to Coinvest. Then make sure paper trading is enabled so nothing here is real money.
  1. Test the connection. Text your bot: "Hey, what's in my portfolio right now?" You should get back a mock portfolio — stocks, crypto, gold, the works. That means you're connected.
  1. Set up your live dashboard habit. Ask Hermes: "Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now?" This is the exact standing instruction Samin gives his real bot — every time it responds and does something, it builds a dashboard and hands you the link.
  1. Get your strategy from QuiverQuant. Log into your QuiverQuant account. Here you can see all congressional trading — which sitting politicians made which trades. (Context from the video: Elizabeth Warren has a $67M net worth on a $285K salary — that's 225 years of saving every paycheck. Nancy Pelosi has outperformed the stock market by a massive margin.) Samin's favorite spot is the Strategies folder — it tracks traders like Michael Burry and Bill Ackman too, and you can filter performance by 1 month, 3 months, or 6 months. Some strategies show a 57.8% return over the last three months. The play: copy-trade them.
  1. Hook up the QuiverQuant MCP. In QuiverQuant, copy your API token (keep it private — don't screen-share it), then go to MCP servers and copy that setup too. Back in Telegram, send it to Hermes along with your API key:
Copy-paste prompt
Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY]

Once connected, Hermes will confirm it can pull real congressional trade data, follow specific politicians, copy-trade QuiverQuant's built-in strategies (like Congress Buy), run deeper research, and set up recurring jobs.

  1. Pick a trader to mirror. Go back to QuiverQuant's Strategies folder and find someone performing well over the last three months. Samin picks Tim Moore. Pick whoever looks strong when you watch.
  1. Launch the full bot. Send Hermes the big one:
Copy-paste prompt
Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of Tim Moore. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading Tim Moore, we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project.
  1. Verify the build. Hermes should confirm: a $10K paper portfolio copy-trading Tim Moore via the QuiverQuant MCP, a daily polling cron, a Friday dashboard cron, and trades mirrored with a 1-3 day disclosure lag. Open the here.now dashboard link and check that it's live.

That's the whole bot. If you ever want to go live with real money, you can flip paper trading off in Coinvest — but run the paper experiment first and let the results talk.

Copy-paste prompts

Connect Coinvest:

Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here.

Test it:

Hey, what's in my portfolio right now?

Dashboard habit:

Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now?

Connect QuiverQuant:

Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY]

Launch the bot:

Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of [TRADER NAME]. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading [TRADER NAME], we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project.

Troubleshooting

Your API key is visible in the chat. After sending your QuiverQuant key to Hermes, Samin deletes that message from Telegram so the key isn't sitting in the thread. Do the same — and never show your token on a screenshot or screen-share.

Double-check paper trading is on. Before you send any trading prompts, confirm paper trading is enabled in Coinvest. Everything in this lesson should be fake money until you deliberately decide otherwise.

✅ Your Day 15 post

Post a screenshot of your live here.now trading dashboard showing your $10K paper account and the trader you're copy-trading — and tell us who you picked and why.

rag.search_text:
Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot

Day 15 — Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Build a Self-Improving Trading Bot What you'll have by the end: An automated paper-trading bot that copy-trades a proven strategy with $10,000 in fake money — polling QuiverQuant for new trades every day, mirroring them through Coinvest, and publishing a live portfolio dashboard on here.now every Friday.

 Why this matters: Hermes learns from its own past trades, so its knowledge compounds — the bot literally gets better the longer it runs. And because this is paper trading, you get all the reps with zero risk.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps A trading bot only needs two services: a broker (Coinvest) and a strategy (QuiverQuant). Hermes glues them together and runs the cron jobs. Here's the exact build.

 - Set up your broker. Go to CoInvest.ai and log in — it's free to make an account. Coinvest lets you trade stocks, crypto, forex, even prediction markets (yes, you can bet on the World Cup), all in one platform built for agentic trading.
 - Grab the connection info. On Coinvest, hit Start on Claude and copy what it gives you (this is the OAuth setup for the Coinvest MCP).
 - Connect it through Telegram. Open the multi-channel Telegram chat you set up earlier and go to your trading topic. Samin does this in Telegram on purpose — you want to be nimble, texting your bot and getting alerts on the go. Paste what you copied and send:
 Copy-paste prompt
Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here. - Authorize it. A window pops up — hit Connect to Coinvest . Then make sure paper trading is enabled so nothing here is real money.
 - Test the connection. Text your bot: "Hey, what's in my portfolio right now?" You should get back a mock portfolio — stocks, crypto, gold, the works. That means you're connected.
 - Set up your live dashboard habit. Ask Hermes: "Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now?" This is the exact standing instruction Samin gives his real bot — every time it responds and does something, it builds a dashboard and hands you the link.
 - Get your strategy from QuiverQuant. Log into your QuiverQuant account. Here you can see all congressional trading — which sitting politicians made which trades. (Context from the video: Elizabeth Warren has a $67M net worth on a $285K salary — that's 225 years of saving every paycheck. Nancy Pelosi has outperformed the stock market by a massive margin.) Samin's favorite spot is the Strategies folder — it tracks traders like Michael Burry and Bill Ackman too, and you can filter performance by 1 month, 3 months, or 6 months. Some strategies show a 57.8% return over the last three months. The play: copy-trade them.
 - Hook up the QuiverQuant MCP. In QuiverQuant, copy your API token (keep it private — don't screen-share it), then go to MCP servers and copy that setup too. Back in Telegram, send it to Hermes along with your API key:
 Copy-paste prompt
Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY] Once connected, Hermes will confirm it can pull real congressional trade data, follow specific politicians, copy-trade QuiverQuant's built-in strategies (like Congress Buy), run deeper research, and set up recurring jobs.

 - Pick a trader to mirror. Go back to QuiverQuant's Strategies folder and find someone performing well over the last three months. Samin picks Tim Moore . Pick whoever looks strong when you watch.
 - Launch the full bot. Send Hermes the big one:
 Copy-paste prompt
Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of Tim Moore. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading Tim Moore, we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project. - Verify the build. Hermes should confirm: a $10K paper portfolio copy-trading Tim Moore via the QuiverQuant MCP, a daily polling cron, a Friday dashboard cron, and trades mirrored with a 1-3 day disclosure lag. Open the here.now dashboard link and check that it's live.
 That's the whole bot. If you ever want to go live with real money, you can flip paper trading off in Coinvest — but run the paper experiment first and let the results talk.

 Copy-paste prompts Connect Coinvest: 

 Can you help me connect to this? This is the OAuth for Coinvest. Help me connect the MCP here. Test it: 

 Hey, what's in my portfolio right now? Dashboard habit: 

 Can you make an HTML dashboard and put it on here.now? Connect QuiverQuant: 

 Hey, can you connect my QuiverQuant MCP with my Hermes agent? Here's my API key: [YOUR API KEY] Launch the bot: 

 Set up a paper trading account and copy the trades of [TRADER NAME]. Siphon off $10,000 in that portfolio and let's see what happens. Every week, make a here.now dashboard and give me an update. Make me a here.now dashboard now, so we're starting it off, with all the data on what's happening right now. Write that we're copy trading [TRADER NAME], we have a balance, and we're starting with $10,000. Make any trades when he makes a trade — it's okay if there's a time differential. Set up a cron job every day during trading hours to check QuiverQuant for updates, and every Friday give me an update. Make all those cron jobs, and inside Multica also make the autopilot so I can track it and the project. Troubleshooting Your API key is visible in the chat. After sending your QuiverQuant key to Hermes, Samin deletes that message from Telegram so the key isn't sitting in the thread. Do the same — and never show your token on a screenshot or screen-share.

 Double-check paper trading is on. Before you send any trading prompts, confirm paper trading is enabled in Coinvest. Everything in this lesson should be fake money until you deliberately decide otherwise.

 ✅ Your Day 15 post Post a screenshot of your live here.now trading dashboard showing your $10K paper account and the trader you're copy-trading — and tell us who you picked and why.

pages.title: Your Personal AI Health Coach
pages.folder: Day 16 — Health Coach & Jarvis Preview
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Your Personal AI Health Coach What you'll have by the end: A Hermes health coach that remembers your blood work and protocols, tracks daily habits through voice notes, and follows up automatically with recurring reminders.

 Why this matters: Health data usually lives in scattered PDFs, sleep apps, workout logs, and your head. Hermes can turn that context into one system that remembers, nudges, and improves with you.

 Build the system - Give Hermes your baseline. Upload the blood-work PDFs or health reports you are comfortable sharing. Ask it to summarize the important markers and keep the source files tied to the project.
- Design your protocol. Talk through your goals, constraints, current supplements, workouts, sleep, and the advice you have already received from qualified clinicians. Hermes should organize the plan, not replace medical care.
- Create recurring check-ins. Ask Hermes to schedule daily reminders for workouts, supplements, sleep, and any other habits you want tracked.
- Report by voice. Send quick voice notes from your phone. Hermes can log the update, identify what is missing, and follow up when you skip something.
- Review the dashboard. Keep one current view of your protocol, adherence, open questions, and the next actions you want to discuss with a qualified professional.
 Copy-paste prompt Use the health information I provide to create a personal tracking system. Organize my baseline, current protocol, supplements, workouts, sleep, and open questions. Set up recurring check-ins, let me report progress by voice note, and follow up when I miss a habit. Keep medical uncertainties clearly labeled and tell me when I should consult a qualified clinician. Safety: This is an organization and accountability workflow, not medical diagnosis or treatment. Do not change medication or clinical care based only on an AI response.

 ✅ Your Day 16 post: Share the one habit you want Hermes to track back, plus the check-in schedule you chose.

🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 Jarvis Mode: Talk to Your AI Empire What you'll have by the end 

 You'll have seen the finished vision of everything you've been building: one command center (Agent Club) that manages every agent and chat you own, plus a live demo of Jarvis mode — a voice-activated agent that speaks your morning briefing out loud. This chapter is a demo, not a build — your job today is to watch it work and pick what you want yours to do.

 Why this matters 

 Everything you've built so far — Hermes, your schedules, your channels — has lived in separate windows. This is what it looks like when it all comes together in one place you can talk to.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 This chapter is a guided demo. Follow along and take notes on the features you want, because Samin teases the full Jarvis build tutorial at the end.

 - See the problem Agent Club solves. Samin shows his screens: he used to chat with Hermes in one place, Codex in another, and Claude Code in a third. Agent Club (built inside the club) is one destination where he manages all of his chats and agents together.
 - Notice the remote access. On the Agent Club screen there's a QR code. Scan it with your phone and you can remote into the same command center. Samin also has it as an app on his phone, and it connects to all the channels you set up in earlier chapters. It has access to a lot, including Hermes.
 - Watch Jarvis mode activate. This is the most requested feature. Jarvis mode starts your day, plans your day, and lets you talk to it. It activates when Samin says a keyword — once triggered, it can control his computer, open things up on screen, and give him a full rundown.
 - Listen to the voice demo. Samin says the wake-up line out loud:
 Copy-paste prompt (say it out loud once your Jarvis is built):
Good morning, Jarvis. What's my day looking like? Jarvis answers back with a full spoken briefing: deep work at 9:30 to finish the Hermes course, Skool community replies at 11, new member intros and unanswered posts at 1:15, a heads-up about a friend's birthday at 7 (so wrap the day around 5), and the top goal for the day broken into three steps.

 - Catch the part where Jarvis already did work overnight. Jarvis reports that it already started the scripts for Samin's Fable YouTube video — they're sitting in his project folder — and it wrote the reel scripts too, so he can start filming whenever he's ready. That's the endgame: an agent that works while you sleep and reports in the morning.
 - Look at the on-screen layout. After the voice demo, Samin points out the left-hand side: his agenda plus the top three things he should be doing today. Because he activated Jarvis that day, it also pulled up his calendar — color-coded, organized, and adjusted around the birthday commute.
 - Note the fun extras. Samin voice-cloned Paul Bettany (the voice of Jarvis in the movies) and put it on his Jarvis, just for fun. He also points out you can build this and even sell it to someone if you want. The full step-by-step build — there are a lot of moving pieces — is coming in the Jarvis tutorial, so keep going.
 Copy-paste prompts 

 The one reusable line from this chapter is the wake-up phrase from the demo:

 Good morning, Jarvis. What's my day looking like? Save it — you'll use it when you build your own Jarvis in the upcoming tutorial.

 ✅ Your Day 16 post 

 Post the one thing you want YOUR Jarvis to say in your morning briefing (for example: "flag my mom's birthday and read me my top 3 goals") — bonus points if you include what you'd have it finish overnight.

pages.title: How I Built Jarvis (And How You Can)
pages.folder: Day 17 — How I Built Jarvis (And How You Can)
course_path: Day 17 — How I Built Jarvis (And How You Can)
pages.text:
🎬 Watch the video above , then follow the steps below.

 How I Built Jarvis (And How You Can) What you'll have by the end: The complete Jarvis recipe — all five components mapped to things you've already built in this course — plus the shortcut: a pre-packaged Jarvis waiting for you in the Skool community.

 Why this matters: Jarvis looks like magic, but it's really just five pieces you already know snapped together. Once you see the recipe, you can build it — and you could even sell it to someone else.

 Watch the video, then follow these steps 

 Jarvis is the same Hermes you've been building all course — your AI chief of staff on your Mac. The upgrades are a good-looking interface, a cloned voice that talks back to you, calendar access, and context on your life. Here's how the five ingredients stack up.

 - Ingredient 1: Life context with Honcho. This is the memory you set up early in the course. Because Hermes knows what's important to you and what you need to be doing — like the top three things Samin tells it before bed — it can surface those in your morning briefing. If your Honcho memory is running, this piece is already done.
 - Ingredient 2: Calendar via Composio + your GCal MCP. With Composio and the Google Calendar MCP connected, Jarvis can automatically organize your calendar. Again — you built this connection earlier. Check it off.
 - Ingredient 3: Computer use. You set this up early on too. Computer use is how Hermes opens tabs and applications on your computer — it has access to Chrome and your apps, so it can open things up and show you what it needs to show you.
 - Ingredient 4: The voice. Samin's voice was done through ElevenLabs (he even cloned Paul Bettany's voice for fun). If you don't want to use ElevenLabs and want to keep it local, use Voicebox instead. Either way, both are driven through Hermes Voice , which is already built in — you just give it your key and it handles the rest. Follow the on-screen steps in the video for where the key goes.
 - Ingredient 5: The HUD (the interface). This comes last because the four pieces above are the core of everything. To build the look, take screenshots of a vibe you like, add what's important to you, and hand it to Claude Design to set the look. Give it your screenshot with a prompt like this:
 Copy-paste prompt 

 Here's a screenshot of a vibe I like. Design an interface for me. - Build it with Fable through your squad. Once Claude Design has the look, get Fable to build it. The trick: use your Hermes as the orchestrator. Go back to your squads (remember, Hermes is your leader). Whenever you have a design project or something really intensive, hand it off to Claude — tell Hermes something like "hey, use Claude to do this." From there it uses Fable on high mode to build and plan everything, and it can use Codex for some of the coding work.
 - Put it together. Now your Jarvis is connected to Hermes — it has a voice and its own computer, and it can speak back and forth with you. That's the whole machine.
 Troubleshooting 

 Be honest with yourself here: this build is not trivial . Samin says it straight — it took a lot of time, a lot of tokens, and plenty of debugging.
